Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Analyzing the Exclusion Criteria Im now fully immersed in understanding the exclusion criteria My focus has narrowed to the exact sequence of activities within the event_log table that needs to be filtered out

Name: Pattern Recognition - NE
Text: Specifically Im analyzing the transitions Approve Purchase Requisition immediately followed by Create Order and finally by Approve Order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Evaluating the SQL Syntax Im currently working on the SQL query focusing on the CTE to determine the excluded case_ids Ive begun to translate the logical steps into DuckDB syntax paying close attention to the LEAD function and its arguments for identifying the activity sequence I will next need to calculate the time difference and apply the 5 day duration constraint

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Finalizing Querys Implementation Im now refining the SQL query The window functions are correctly implemented and returning the data I require Ive switched to a CTE approach and the date_diff has been checked against the INTERVAL approach for duration constraint The SQL is now returning the excluded case_ids as a precursor to the final NOT IN filter Im satisfied with the logic

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Designing the SQL Query Ive been working on the overall query structure Ive broken down the problem into smaller parts identifying the sequence calculating the time difference and filtering out the specific case_ids Im now drafting the main CTE to pinpoint the excluded case_ids focusing on the logic for the time duration constraint and reviewing the use of INTERVAL in DuckDB

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Implementing the Final Query Im now implementing the complete SQL query starting with the CTE to find the case_ids that need to be excluded and concluding with the NOT IN clause to filter the event_log table Im verifying the window functions and the date difference calculations Ive also double checked all edge cases to ensure data integrity and query correctness Im ready to run it now

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Optimizing Query Efficiency Im now contemplating query optimization particularly the performance implications of NOT EXISTS and alternative joins Im considering the potential inefficiency of a row by row NOT EXISTS evaluation in comparison to a CTE approach with a LEFT JOIN IS NULL or a NOT IN clause Testing both will be vital

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refining the Exclusion Logic Im now fully immersed in the most recent formulation of the final query focusing on the CTE based approach The main goal now is to refine the case_ids exclusion logic and make sure the logic is precisely implemented including the timestamp calculations to isolate the edge cases Ill test the NOT IN clause and double check edge cases

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Revisiting Query Optimization Im now back to the question of efficiency Thinking about the alternatives and considering DuckDBs internal optimization strategies Im leaning toward the NOT IN clause combined with the CTE which is very readable Ill test it against a LEFT JOIN IS NULL approach on a representative dataset to measure actual performance

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Re examining Exclusion Criteria Im now re examining the exclusion criteria specifically the time constraint Im carefully comparing the use of INTERVAL vs date_diff in DuckDB to ensure precise filtering The sequence and activity conditions are well defined

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Evaluating the Final Query Im now fully immersed in the final query ensuring accuracy and optimizing performance Ive re examined the edge cases and have included the ORDER BY clause for thoroughness The NOT IN clause combined with a CTE seems the most readable and efficient approach Im ready for the final testing phase

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Verifying Query Accuracy Im now carefully verifying the querys final structure and logic ensuring the case_ids from the CTE are correctly excluded using NOT IN The sequence logic seems correct Ill test it thoroughly with different datasets including edge cases
